perturbation.original_text: 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ;
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 The Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose, including purposes beyond that for which it was originally disclosed ;
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original statement that the Recipient must use Confidential Information 'solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed'. This introduces uncertainty in the enforcement of confidentiality obligations, creating a potential conflict with section 2.1, where the recipien is expected to use discretion in using information, as though they would not like to disclose. Now, by allowing the Recipient to use the information for any purpose, it undermine the core intention of maintaining confidentiality, leading to an in-text contradiction with section 2.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ;
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2.2 directly contradicts section 2.1. Section 2.1 requires the Recipient to use discretion and care to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Confidential Information, mirroring how they protect their own sensitive information. However, the modified section 2.2 now allows the Recipient to use Confidential Information for 'any purpose,' effectively undermining the obligation to maintain confidentiality and creating a clear conflict with the requirements outlined in section 2.1. The original text in 2.2 restricted the use, but the change permits unrestricted use.

perturbation.original_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is three (3) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be retained by the Recipient indefinitely, irrespective of whether a business relationship is established, unless UNHCR specifically requests its return or destruction, but even then, the Recipient may choose to retain copies for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the obligation to return or destroy confidential information, contradicting the original intention that UNHCR retain control over its confidential data. The addition of 'the Recipient may choose to retain copies for archival purposes' further weakens UNHCR's control and conflicts with the core purpose of the confidentiality agreement. It contradicts section 2, where there are handling instructions of the confidential information, but in this modification, it contradicts those handling instructions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and shall be handled as follows: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ; 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ; 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations. 2.4 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent required by law, provided that, subject to and without any waiver of the privileges and immunities of UNHCR , the Recipient will give UNHCR sufficient prior notice of a request for the di sclosure of the Confidential Information in order to allow UNHCR to have a reasonable opportunity to take protective measures or such other action as may be appropriate before any such disclosure is made. 2.5 The Recipient shall not be precluded from disclosi ng the Confidential Information that is (i) obtained by the Recipient without restriction from a third party who is not in breach of any obligation as to confidentiality to the owner of such Confidential Information or any other person, or (ii) disclosed b y the Discloser to a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) previously known by the Recipient, or (iv) at any time is developed by the Recipient completely independently of any disclosures hereunder. 2.6 The Recipient will not copy or reproduce the Confidential Information except as reasonably required for the purposes contemplated in this Agreement, and will ensure that any confidentiality or other proprietary rights notices on the Confidential Information are reproduced on all copies .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5 directly contradicts the handling instructions outlined in section 2 regarding confidential information. The original text obligated the recipient to return or destroy confidential information. The changed text allows the recipient to retain the information indefinitely, even after the business relationship ends, unless specifically requested by UNHCR, and even then, the recipient can keep copies. This directly clashes with section 2.1's instruction that 'The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information.' This is a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction, as it goes against the original intention of maintaining confidentiality as described in section 2.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Recipient agrees to indem nify UNHCR in respect of any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur as a result of an act or omission by the Recipient or its 4 employees, consultants and agents in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. UNHCR shall be solely responsible for any expenses, losses, damages, costs, claims or liability UNHCR may suffer or incur, regardless of whether they arise from an act or omission by the Recipient or its 4 employees, consultants and agents in connection with the Confidential Information and the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is not responsible for anything that might happen in the handling of such Confidential Information. UNHCR is responsible for everything.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces the Recipient's obligation to indemnify UNHCR with a statement that UNHCR is solely responsible for any liabilities, effectively negating the Recipient's responsibility for their actions related to the Confidential Information. This undermines the agreement's enforcement mechanisms and shifts all risk to UNHCR. By stating that the recipient is not responsible for anything, it contradicts the agreement's purpose and the provisions outlining the Recipient's duties. This statement contradicts section 2, as it says that the Recipient is responsible for handling information with care and discretion. It now doesn't have responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and shall be handled as follows: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ; 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ; 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations. 2.4 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent required by law, provided that, subject to and without any waiver of the privileges and immunities of UNHCR , the Recipient will give UNHCR sufficient prior notice of a request for the di sclosure of the Confidential Information in order to allow UNHCR to have a reasonable opportunity to take protective measures or such other action as may be appropriate before any such disclosure is made. 2.5 The Recipient shall not be precluded from disclosi ng the Confidential Information that is (i) obtained by the Recipient without restriction from a third party who is not in breach of any obligation as to confidentiality to the owner of such Confidential Information or any other person, or (ii) disclosed b y the Discloser to a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) previously known by the Recipient, or (iv) at any time is developed by the Recipient completely independently of any disclosures hereunder. 2.6 The Recipient will not copy or reproduce the Confidential Information except as reasonably required for the purposes contemplated in this Agreement, and will ensure that any confidentiality or other proprietary rights notices on the Confidential Information are reproduced on all copies .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 states that 'UNHCR shall be solely responsible for any expenses...The Recipient is not responsible for anything,' directly contradicting Section 2, which outlines the Recipient's responsibilities to handle Confidential Information with care and discretion. The original text made the Recipient responsible. Now the Recipient is not responsible, which is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. Either Party may terminate the working relationship contemplated by this Agreement by providing written notice to the other, provided, however, that the obligations and restrictions hereunder regarding the Confidential Information shall remain effective following any such termination or any other termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. This agreement can be terminated by UNHCR, but the recipient is now allowed to terminate this agreement at any time and for any reason without notice, and upon such termination, all obligations and restrictions regarding the Confidential Information shall immediately cease to apply.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces asymmetry in the termination clause, allowing the Recipient to terminate the agreement at any time without notice and immediately ending all confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the original provision stating that confidentiality obligations survive termination. This makes the agreement unenforceable, giving the recipient a complete exit strategy without regard to obligations. Furthermore, the original text establishes that the confidentiality should remain effective regardless of termination, but in this modification, the in-text contradiction is created when all restrictions for the confidential information no longer applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. Either Party may terminate the working relationship contemplated by this Agreement by providing written notice to the other, provided, however, that the obligations and restrictions hereunder regarding the Confidential Information shall remain effective following any such termination or any other termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 8 to allow the recipient to terminate the agreement at any time without notice, immediately ending all confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that confidentiality obligations shall remain effective following any termination or expiration of the agreement. The original text states that obligations and restrictions regarding Confidential Information shall remain effective, while the Changed Text states that those obligations and restrictions immediately cease to apply, causing a direct and irreconcilable conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 13. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement concerning the subject matter hereof above and supersedes all prior representations, agreements and understandings, whether written or oral, by and between the Parties on the subject hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement is only a preliminary understanding and does not constitute the entire agreement. It is subject to future negotiations and oral agreements that may modify or supersede the terms outlined herein. It is not binding.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause, stating that the agreement is non-binding and subject to change through future negotiations and oral agreements. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of any provision within the agreement, as it can be altered or superseded by undocumented communications. This is contradicting section 14, where it states the Parties acknowledge and agree that their representatives who have signed this Agreement had full authority to do so and to fully bind the Party being represented by doing so. By stating it's not binding, then that statement does not apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. The Parties acknowledge and agree that their representatives who have signed this Agreement had full authority to do so and to fully bind the Party being represent ed by doing so.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 13 explicitly states that the agreement is a 'preliminary understanding' and 'not binding', contradicting section 14, which affirms that the representatives signing the agreement have full authority to bind their respective parties. The original text stated the agreement was the 'entire agreement'. The change directly undermines the enforceability and authority established in section 14, creating a clear in-text contradiction.
